summary_one_line: DeviceClass FOURCC at bytes 0x0c-0x0f must equal 'prtr' (cmsSigOutputClass) to take the case-arm true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte profile DeviceClass field at input offsets 0x0c-0x0f must spell the FOURCC 'prtr' (0x70 0x72 0x74 0x72 = cmsSigOutputClass). The switch in validDeviceClass routes to the line-751 case-arm only when cl == 'prtr'; loser inputs carry a different but valid FOURCC ('mntr' = cmsSigDisplayClass) that resolves at line 750 instead, never hitting the line-751 true side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed has bytes 70 72 74 72 ('prtr') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f; the BYTE DIFF concentrates W at 0x0c=70(p)x11 and 0x0d=72(r)x11, and the DIVERGENT BRANCHES show the case cmsSigOutputClass arm taken by W (T=11 F=0) while case cmsSigDisplayClass is not.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has bytes 6d 6e 74 72 ('mntr') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f; the BYTE DIFF concentrates L at 0x0c=6d(m)x20 and 0x0d=6e(n)x20, so the switch routes them to case cmsSigDisplayClass (L 750 T=20 F=0) and the line-751 true side stays blocked (L 751 T=0 F=20).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback: the switch on cl compiles to integer CMPs against the FOURCC constants; each CMP callback writes a Hamming-distance bucket into CMP_MAP, so an input whose DeviceClass dword is closer to 'prtr' than any seen before adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus, letting VP ratchet the field from 'mntr' toward 'prtr' byte by byte. naive and cmplog (without the CMP_MAP distance signal) have no gradient toward the exact 4-byte literal and stall on the unrelated valid class 'mntr'.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case cmsSigOutputClass arm of the switch in validDeviceClass.
The blocked side is reached only via the switch on the DeviceClass value cl.
Winner inputs concentrate byte 0x70 ('p') at offset 0x0c and 0x72 ('r') at offset 0x0d while losers concentrate 0x6d ('m') and 0x6e ('n').
Offset 0x0d also differs decisively, W='r' vs L='n'.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the FOURCC 'prtr' at the DeviceClass offset.
Loser-blocking seeds carry the different valid FOURCC 'mntr' at the DeviceClass offset.
Winner takes the line-751 case true side; loser takes the line-750 case instead, leaving 751 blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 751  T=11 F=0  T=0 F=20  case cmsSigOutputClass:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming-distance bucket per CMP, preserving inputs whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile is the winner over both naive and cmplog with the technique delta being value_profile.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile distance feedback), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S-floor lineage check does not apply. The byte-level evidence (W='prtr' vs L='mntr' at 0x0c-0x0f, four-byte FOURCC match against the switch constant) and the value_profile-vs-naive technique delta on both decisive pairs are the basis; no dash-row lineage query was run because the dash signal is an I2S floor, irrelevant to a VP-gradient claim.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating a case-arm on an exact 4-byte FOURCC equality where value_profile fails to resolve it any faster than naive (no dose-response on the distance-to-literal axis), which would show CMP_MAP gradient gives no advantage on this comparison shape.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the FOURCC switch compiles to a distance-bucketable integer CMP that VP can ratchet (vs a jump table that emits no gradient-friendly CMP) is inferred from source, not confirmed against the compiled CMP_MAP behavior.
